title: Why Enter does not work
checkLabels.t4: The first sentence is inside a paragraph
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the page instead of wrapping the sentences. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed:
A

is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed: Wrapping is two things. It is easy to put the opening tag in and forget the closing one.
hint3.not-closed: Hello there — that is not a paragraph. That is a paragraph that never ends. The browser swallows everything after it.
hint4.not-closed:
Count the opening

tags and the closing

tags. The numbers must match.
why.no-p:
There are not three paragraphs. Each of the three sentences wants its OWN

— not one shared wrapper.
hint2.no-p: Count the paragraphs. You need three, because there are three sentences.
hint3.no-p:
One shared

around all three does not do it — that is one paragraph with three sentences, not three paragraphs. The blank lines inside it mean nothing, you have seen that already.
hint4.no-p: Close each paragraph before you open the next one. Three opening tags, three closing tags.
why.empty-p:
There is an empty

. An empty paragraph is not a paragraph — it carries nothing. Either put text in it or remove it.
hint2.empty-p: You opened and closed a paragraph with nothing between them. The text stayed outside.
hint3.empty-p:
Hello — the tag is empty and Hello hangs outside it. It should be

Hello

.
hint4.empty-p: The text goes BETWEEN the opening and the closing tag. Not before them, not after them.
why.loose-first:
The first sentence is still bare text. Blank lines do not exist for the browser — you have to SAY that this is a paragraph. Wrap it in

.
hint2.loose-first: Look at where the sentence starts and where it ends. The tag opens before the first letter and closes after the full stop.
hint3.loose-first:
If you have the line Hello there, the paragraph is

Hello there

. You open before the H and close after the e.
hint4.loose-first: Wrap the first sentence. Opening tag in front, closing tag behind. The full stop goes INSIDE.
why.loose-second: The second sentence is still bare text. All three must be wrapped, not just the first.
why.loose-third: The third sentence is still bare text. One left.
why.lost-h1: The heading is gone. The task is to wrap the sentences, not to delete things.
hint2.lost-h1: My favourite game must still be up there, as an <h1>.
blocks.0.text: Look at the code on the right. Three sentences, neatly separated by blank lines. Now look at the result.
blocks.1.text: They are glued together. All in one line, like a single long sentence. The blank lines are gone.
blocks.3.text: Press Enter ten more times. Add twenty blank lines. Run. Nothing. The browser swallows them all.
blocks.4.text: This is not a bug. Blank lines in code are for your eyes — to keep it readable. The browser ignores them on purpose, because otherwise every reshuffle of your code would shift the page.
blocks.7.text:
If you want a separate paragraph — you say so explicitly.

does not mean "new line". It means "this is a standalone paragraph". The spacing comes as a consequence.
blocks.8.text: Wrap the three sentences and run.
blocks.9.text: In HTML nothing happens because you arranged it nicely. It happens because you said so.
